ogTitle: Lane Cove North vs Langley Vale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lane Cove North and Langley Vale.
heading: Lane Cove North vs Langley Vale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lane Cove North, New South Wales and Langley Vale, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Langley Vale has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Lane Cove North (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lane Cove North or Langley Vale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lane Cove North has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Langley Vale scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lane Cove North scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Langley Vale scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lane Cove North or Langley Vale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Langley Vale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
